data.title: Meta's Planned Layoffs Influence Polymarket Predictions
data.description: Polymarket's prediction market, "Tech Layoffs Up or Down in 2026?", saw a significant increase to 85% YES on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, this surge follows reports that Meta intends to reduce its workforce by approximately 8,000 employees. The initial phase of these layoffs is scheduled for May 20. Additionally, Meta's AI capital expenditure guidance for 2026 is projected to be between $115 billion and $135 billion.

data.title: South Korea's KOSDAQ Index Surges Past 1,200 Points
data.description: South Korea's KOSDAQ index experienced a significant rise, reaching 1,200.22, marking an increase of 2.21% from the previous day. According to NS3.AI, this surge pushed the index above the 1,200-point threshold. Meanwhile, the benchmark KOSPI index traded at 6,442.84, reflecting a decline of 0.51% after achieving its all-time high of 6,557.76 the day before.

data.title: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren Seeks Details on Erebor Bank's Regulatory Approval
data.description: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren is seeking further information regarding Erebor Bank, according to Foresight News. The bank, co-founded by a prominent supporter of U.S. President Donald Trump, focuses on cryptocurrency operations and received regulatory approval earlier this year. Warren has expressed significant concerns based on a fundraising document obtained by her office, questioning how the bank secured its license within months and whether political connections expedited the process.

data.title: The state of Wisconsin has sued Kalshi and other platforms, claiming that prediction markets constitute unlicensed gambling.
data.description: Wisconsin Attorney General Josh Kaul has filed a lawsuit against Kalshi, Coinbase, Polymarket, Robinhood, and Crypto.com, alleging that their prediction markets are essentially unlicensed gambling activities. The complaint claims that the platforms' marketing methods and event contract pricing structures meet the state's definition of gambling. This case further exacerbates the conflict between state-level regulation and the federal jurisdiction of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). (CoinDesk)

data.title: Polymarket cracks down on insider trading: If users are found to have traded using classified government information, the case will be handed over to the Department of Justice and an investigation will be conducted.
data.description: Polymarket stated in a post on its X platform, "Last month, we released enhanced market integrity rules to combat insider trading. When we discover users using classified government information for trading, we refer the matter to the Department of Justice and cooperate with their investigation. Polymarket has zero tolerance for insider trading, and today's arrest proves the effectiveness of this system." The arrest Polymarket referred to was on Thursday when U.S. federal authorities arrested a special forces soldier involved in the arrest of Venezuelan President Maduro. The soldier is suspected of profiting over $400,000 by betting on Maduro's removal from power. Sources say federal investigators believe the commando placed over $33,000 on the prediction market Polymarket hours before President Trump announced Maduro's arrest in January. For details on insider trading on Polymarket related to the Maduro arrest operation, see "When War is Settled Before News: How Prediction Markets 'Priced' the Maduro Arrest 6 Days in Advance."

data.title: Polymarket's Enhanced Integrity Rules Lead to Arrest Over Insider Trading
data.description:
Polymarket announced on the X platform that it has implemented strengthened market integrity rules to combat insider trading. According to Odaily, the company stated that when users are found trading with government confidential information, the matter is referred to the Department of Justice for investigation. Polymarket emphasized its zero tolerance for insider trading, noting that recent arrests demonstrate the effectiveness of these measures.

The arrest mentioned by Polymarket involved U.S. federal authorities detaining a special forces soldier on Thursday. The soldier was implicated in profiting over $400,000 by betting on the ousting of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. Sources revealed that federal investigators suspect the soldier placed a bet exceeding $33,000 on Polymarket just hours before U.S. President Donald Trump announced Maduro's capture in January.

For more details on the insider trading related to Maduro's capture on Polymarket, refer to the article "When War Settles Before News: How Prediction Markets Priced Maduro's Capture Six Days in Advance."

data.title: Space Economy Enters New Era with SpaceX's Epic IPO in 2026
data.description: In 2026, space is no longer just a government laboratory but has become a financial hub for Wall Street. According to PANews, with SpaceX launching its monumental IPO, the space economy officially enters the 'valuation reconstruction' era.

data.title: Wisconsin Challenges Legality of Prediction Market Contracts
data.description: Wisconsin has initiated legal action against five companies and their partners, alleging that their prediction market contracts constitute unlicensed gambling products. According to NS3.AI, the complaints argue that these contracts allow users to wager money on real-world events, such as NCAA tournament games, with the potential to receive a fixed payout of $1 if their predictions are correct. The lawsuits intensify the ongoing debate over whether prediction market contracts should be regulated under state gambling laws or fall under the jurisdiction of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).

data.title: Japan's Central Bank to Maintain Current Policy Amid Middle East Tensions, Economist Says
data.description: Economist Nobuyasu Atago from Rakuten Securities Economic Research Institute has highlighted the key focus for the Bank of Japan's next move. According to Jin10, Atago noted that the rise in energy prices due to Middle East conflicts could trigger a chain reaction affecting other goods and services. He anticipates that the Bank of Japan will maintain its current policy at next week's meeting, emphasizing the importance of providing necessary funds to those in need rather than raising interest rates. Atago stated, 'Delaying a rate hike is appropriate until the situation in the Strait of Hormuz normalizes or the outlook for crude oil procurement becomes clear.'

data.title: Nike Announces Global Workforce Reduction
data.description:
Nike announced on Thursday that approximately 1,400 employees from its global operations team will be laid off. According to BlockBeats, this decision was reported by Fox News on April 24.

In a memo to employees, Chief Operating Officer Venkatesh Alagirisamy stated that the layoffs will primarily affect the company's technology department across North America, Asia, and Europe, representing less than 2% of Nike's total global workforce.

Alagirisamy noted, "This is not a new direction but the next phase of ongoing work." The announcement comes as Nike restructures its business through a series of layoffs.

data.title: Hyperliquid Trader Increases Leverage on CHIP Holdings
data.description:
On April 24, Hyperinsight reported that Hyperliquid trader 'neoyokio.eth' increased their leveraged long position on CHIP, raising the position size from $2.2 million to $3.4 million. According to BlockBeats, the average cost price was adjusted to $0.098, with a current unrealized loss of approximately $120,000, or 11%.

The account currently holds long positions in four cryptocurrencies: ASTER, CHIP, MON, and TAO, with a total value of $24.8 million. ASTER represents the largest position, with an average price of $0.636, making it the largest long position on Hyperliquid for ASTER.

Address: 0x15271757b398cd1d3d7cca05c4f7b0c159afa7c2

data.title: MegaETH has officially announced its TGE (Tencent Token Offering) on ​​April 30th, and Polymarket currently reports a 95% probability of issuance on that day.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that MegaETH announced today that it will conduct a TGE on April 30th, and the probability of "MegaETH launching its token before April 30th" on Polymarket is currently 95%, which may be related to the settlement rules. The market's settlement rules state that if MegaETH officially launches its governance token before 11:59 PM Eastern Time on the date specified in the title, the prediction market result will be judged as "yes"; otherwise, it will be judged as "no". Another key rule, "the token must be publicly transferable and tradable; merely issuing a related announcement does not meet the judgment conditions," may explain why the probability did not reach 100%. Odaily Seer continues to monitor prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Spark Sees $2.4 Billion Net Inflows as Capital Exits Aave
data.description: Spark has experienced significant net inflows totaling approximately $2.4 billion, according to NS3.AI. This movement of capital comes as funds are being withdrawn from Aave. Notably, at least 20 addresses have each deposited over $20 million into Spark, highlighting a substantial shift in investment.

data.title: The 10-day streak of net inflows ended yesterday, with the Ethereum spot ETF experiencing a total net outflow of $75.936 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $75.936 million yesterday (April 23rd, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $19.7581 million. ETH's historical total net inflow has reached $1.926 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $51.2987 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.341 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $13.713 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.89%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.979 billion.

data.title: Charles Schwab Introduces Direct Bitcoin and Ether Trading on Brokerage Platform
data.description: Charles Schwab has initiated a phased rollout of direct spot trading for Bitcoin and Ether within its brokerage platform, which manages $11.9 trillion in client assets. According to NS3.AI, Schwab's Q4 2025 earnings report indicated 38.5 million active brokerage accounts. The custody of these digital assets is managed by Charles Schwab Premier Bank, while Paxos handles the execution of trades. The service imposes a fee of 75 basis points per trade. It is important to note that cryptocurrencies held through Schwab are not protected by FDIC deposit insurance or SIPC protection.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Stocks Show Mixed Performance as Tech Stocks Rebound
data.description:
On April 24, Hong Kong's major stock indices opened lower but showed a recovery trend by midday, according to Jin10. The Hang Seng Tech Index turned positive just before the midday break. By the close of the morning session, the Hang Seng Index had fallen by 0.2%, while the Hang Seng Tech Index rose by 0.34%.

Some previously declining heavyweight tech stocks showed signs of recovery. Baidu (09888.HK) saw a sharp increase of over 2%, while Alibaba (09988.HK) also turned positive. Tencent Holdings (00700.HK), Xiaomi Group (01810.HK), and JD.com (09618.HK) experienced declines of less than 1%.

Semiconductor stocks showed significant gains, with Hua Hong Semiconductor (01347.HK) leading the rise. Additionally, stocks in the oil sector, brain-computer interface concept stocks, and robotics concept stocks also saw increases.

data.title: Spark Introduces Comprehensive Risk Management Features
data.description: Spark has unveiled a detailed overview of its risk management features. According to PANews, Spark Savings Vault is supported by multiple layers of first-loss capital and liquidity protection. Additionally, SparkLend's currency market employs multiple oracles and rate limits to safeguard user assets.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $223 million yesterday, marking the seventh consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $223 million yesterday (April 23rd, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest single-day net inflow with $167 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $167 million. Following closely were Ark Invest and 21Shares' ARKB ETF, with a combined net inflow of $71.2238 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.619 billion. Conversely, the Fidelity ETF FBTC saw the largest single-day net outflow with $16.9254 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.035 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $102.793 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.59%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $58.213 billion.

data.title: CoinList Partners with Superstate for Blockchain-Based Stock Issuance
data.description: CoinList has announced a partnership with Superstate's blockchain equity issuance platform, Opening Bell. According to Foresight News, this collaboration will enable users to purchase newly issued stocks directly using stablecoins and hold real equity on-chain, allowing for instant and direct settlement. The first batch of issuances is expected to launch in the second quarter of 2026, with a waitlist registration currently open.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Hong Kong AI Stocks Experience Sharp Decline
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market witnessed a significant downturn in AI concept stocks. According to Jin10, MINIMAX-W (00100.HK) saw a sharp drop of nearly 5%, while Zhipu (02513.HK) experienced a decline of over 7% at one point.
